commands.purpose: Print the MFA posture for a User's underlying Identity (which methods are enrolled, credential list, recovery-code count). Every field is redacted-by-default; support runs this to answer 'is MFA enrolled for this user?' without ever seeing secret material. --json outputs machine-readable format for scripting.

Print detail for one method. For webauthn, --credential=<wac_ulid> narrows to one credential. --reveal-metadata exposes the AAGUID + full transports list (normally internal-tier); every reveal is audit-logged with the operator + reason.

Platform-security force-disables MFA for a User during account-recovery flows (lost device + lost recovery codes). Cascades: TOTP secret nulled on Identity, every active WebauthnCredential.disabled_at set to now(), MfaDisabled(trigger=admin_action) event fired. --reason is MANDATORY in production (config('mfa.commands.require_reason')); --notify-user dispatches MfaDisabledConfirmationNotification (default: yes). Audit-critical — every invocation writes to the compliance-audit log with the operator's Identity + reason.
usage_examples: php artisan mfa:force-disable usr_01H... --reason='Support ticket #12345, user lost hardware key + recovery codes, verified user identity via video call'

commands.purpose: Regenerate recovery codes for a user + optionally email them the new codes. Used when a user reports lost recovery codes but retains the TOTP device. --reason mandatory in production; --email defaults on; audit-logged. Plaintext codes are echoed to the terminal ONLY when --email is absent (so support can read them back on a call), otherwise dispatched via email and NEVER printed.
usage_examples: php artisan mfa:regenerate-recovery-codes usr_01H... --reason='Support ticket #12345, user lost recovery codes' --email

commands.purpose: List the User's registered WebAuthn credentials. Default filters to active only (disabled_at IS NULL); --include-disabled surfaces the 90-day retention window. Fields shown: id, device_name, attestation_format, is_platform_authenticator, transports, sign_count, last_used_at, disabled_at.

Run PurgeUnconfirmedEnrollmentsJob synchronously. --dry-run reports counts without deleting. Useful for offline environments where the scheduler isn't running, or for a post-incident cleanup after a rate-limit spike.

Report Identities with N or more MfaChallengeFailed events in the last H hours. Default: 5 failures in 24 hours. --json emits machine-readable output for integration with the incident-response pipeline. Every row includes: identity_fingerprint (SHA-256, never plaintext), failed_count, distinct_ips_count, first_failed_at, last_failed_at, methods_used[]. Useful for pattern-hunt after a suspected credential-stuffing wave.
